title: recursive call in user directories for unit files
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->
Solves #18032 

```release-note
Recursively scans directories for unit files
----------------------
debian@debian:~/Projects/podman$ bin/quadlet -dryrun
quadlet-generator[2453]: No files to parse from [/etc/containers/systemd /usr/share/containers/systemd]

----------------------
debian@debian:~/Projects/podman$ bin/quadlet -dryrun -user
quadlet-generator[2475]: Loading source unit file /home/debian/.config/containers/systemd/test_subdir/sample.container
quadlet-generator[2475]: Loading source unit file /home/debian/.config/containers/systemd/test_subdir/sub_one/sample_two.container
quadlet-generator[2475]: Loading source unit file /home/debian/.config/containers/systemd/test_subdir/sub_two/sample_one.container
---sample.service---
[Unit]
Description=The sleep container
After=local-fs.target
SourcePath=/home/debian/.config/containers/systemd/test_subdir/sample.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[X-Container]
Image=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest
Exec=sleep 1000

[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/local/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest sleep 1000

---sample_two.service---
[Unit]
Description=The sleep container
After=local-fs.target
SourcePath=/home/debian/.config/containers/systemd/test_subdir/sub_one/sample_two.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[X-Container]
Image=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest
Exec=sleep 1000

[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/local/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest sleep 1000

---sample_one.service---
[Unit]
Description=The sleep container
After=local-fs.target
SourcePath=/home/debian/.config/containers/systemd/test_subdir/sub_two/sample_one.container
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[X-Container]
Image=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest
Exec=sleep 1000

[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/local/bin/podman rm -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon -d registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest sleep 1000
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *harikannan512* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM
Because there were many changes since @giuseppe's approval, I would like to get another LGTM before merging. @TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe can you please take another look?
Also I don't see any changes to the documentation.

Does this handle rootful containers correctly?

If I put a quadlet in /etc/containers/systemd/users/1000/myquadlet.container, does this get picked up as a system container?  If it does, then that is a bug.  It should only be used for a user with a UID==1000.

> Does this handle rootful containers correctly?
> 
> If I put a quadlet in /etc/containers/systemd/users/1000/myquadlet.container, does this get picked up as a system container? If it does, then that is a bug. It should only be used for a user with a UID==1000.

Quadlet doesn't run `myquadlet.container` when run as user -> 1000 without `-user`. But when run in sudo or otherwise with `-user`, it does get picked up
When run in sudo:

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/4dc13531-0ab1-417b-8268-91c3c3af2094)

When run as user 1000:

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/76409c5b-76d4-4ee4-83c8-9171742d5191)

> Also I don't see any changes to the documentation.

@Luap99  I have added a couple of lines to the documentation now. If there is a specific expectation, would be happy to know so that I can add to this

> Quadlet doesn't run `myquadlet.container` when run as user -> 1000 without `-user`. But when run in sudo or otherwise with `-user`, it does get picked up

Now I'm concerned about the fact that it does not get picked up (not that it should). How do we explain that adding `/etc/containers/systemd` and all its sub-tree does not add `/etc/containers/systemd/users/1000/` to the list of directories?

> > Quadlet doesn't run `myquadlet.container` when run as user -> 1000 without `-user`. But when run in sudo or otherwise with `-user`, it does get picked up
> 
> Now I'm concerned about the fact that it does not get picked up (not that it should). How do we explain that adding `/etc/containers/systemd` and all its sub-tree does not add `/etc/containers/systemd/users/1000/` to the list of directories?

It shouldn't get picked up.  We probably need to make an exception for the `users` sub-dir?

> It shouldn't get picked up. We probably need to make an exception for the `users` sub-dir?

I agree that it shouldn't. But, according to @harikannan512 to already doesn't which I don't understand why. This makes me think that unless we can explain it, there's a bug that we're missing somewhere

If we want to recursive walk directories under /etc/containers/systemd looking for quadlets, then we need to only look for directories under  /etc/containers/systemd/users/$UID where UID==current UID.  IE If the quadlet generator is running as root it should IGNORE quadlets in /etc/containers/systemd/users/ subdirs not starting with UID=0.

> If we want to recursive walk directories under /etc/containers/systemd looking for quadlets, then we need to only look for directories under /etc/containers/systemd/users/$UID where UID==current UID. IE If the quadlet generator is running as root it should IGNORE quadlets in /etc/containers/systemd/users/ subdirs not starting with UID=0.

Yes, that sounds reasonable.

@harikannan512 I see that you make sure the PR is in tact by rebasing. But, we in order to merge this change, we need to resolve the discussion about the users directories under `/etc/containers/systemd`

> @harikannan512 I see that you make sure the PR is in tact by rebasing. But, we in order to merge this change, we need to resolve the discussion about the users directories under `/etc/containers/systemd` @ygalblum @rhatdan Sure thing. Just to reiterate my understanding: - when quadlet is run as sudo, it should scan `/etc/containers/systemd` and `/usr/share/containers/systemd` directories - when quadlet runs as sudo with `-user` option, it should be able to scan sub-dirs within directories defined by root UIDs (i.e. 0*), but not the other user directories. ![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/06d9d713-9ba0-4a12-ab01-5d1279f37495)

@harikannan512 I think that first, we need to keep in mind that Quadlet is a systemd generator.

If we set aside `dry-run` Quadlet is executed either via `/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator` or `/usr/lib/systemd/user-generators/podman-user-generator` without the `-user` parameter in either case. Quadlet figures out the context using the program name [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/quadlet/main.go#L321), though it might have been preferable to use the [SYSTEMD_SCOPE](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.generator.html#%24SYSTEMD_SCOPE) environment variable. In any case, the option of running as root with `-user` set is not real life use case.

Now, let's look at the current implementation of [getUnitDirs](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/quadlet/main.go#L102). We can see that for `root` it adds two directories: `/etc/containers/systemd` and `/usr/share/containers/systemd` while for rootless, it adds `~/.config/containers/systemd`, `/etc/containers/systemd/users` and `/etc/containers/systemd/users/$UID`.

So, as I see it that are two possible issues:
1. When running as `root` we want to traverse the sub-dirs of `/etc/containers/systemd` but we need to make sure that we don't go into the `users` directory as this sub-tree includes units for user level services that should not be executed for `root`.
2. When running as non-root we need to make sure that when traversing `/etc/containers/systemd/users` we don't go into other users' directory. This one, I guess is a bit more tricky because there is no one directory not to enter to. Maybe enter all directories that are not a number unless the number is the user's UID?

@rhatdan please confirm my analysis to make sure we're not redoing this section over and over again.
Yes I agree with your analysys.

I believe I confused the system superuser with Podman root/admin user. These new changes ensure 

a) If root, quadlet will only run user files outside of `users` folder, and will NOT enter `users` dir, even for user id 0.
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/13f4b8df-3268-4bb1-9dd9-98c55c7d11ab)

b) When rootless, the users folder will be traverses recursively, but only for 
- the current user id
- non numeric sub dirs

User ID: 1000
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/8b124a09-4c9c-4b0c-b2e6-b18fdb37371d)

User ID: 1001
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/6c14e01d-b2c9-4905-89e6-6fe1a652a9d9)

Tree:
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/30907214/9125bfa3-aca7-482e-912f-c345b3180b2f)
@harikannan512 you'll need to rebase on top of main in order to take in the fix for the Windows installer
just a drive by comment but please squash the commits into one before merging

@rhatdan @vrothberg PTAL

Also, was the Rawhide issue resolved? I see many PRs failing on it

> Also, was the Rawhide issue resolved? I see many PRs failing on it

https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/issues/232
Ignore it for now it is not blocking CI.

/lgtm
@harikannan512 Thanks for sticking with this.
